query: IAEA Director General Update ZNPP power loss frequency 2025-2026 timeline
answer:
This is excellent — the sub-agent was able to retrieve comprehensive data. Let me compile the final synthesis.

---

## IAEA Director General Updates: ZNPP Power Loss Frequency 2025–2026 Timeline

### Overview

The Zaporizhzhia Nuclear Power Plant (ZNPP) — Europe's largest nuclear power plant — has experienced **10 confirmed complete losses of external power** since Russian forces occupied it in March 2022. IAEA Director General Rafael Grossi has issued repeated warnings about the escalating danger, with the situation reaching its worst point during a **30-day continuous blackout in September–October 2025**.

---

### Complete Timeline of External Power Loss Events

| # | Date | Duration | Notes |
|---|------|----------|-------|
| 1 | 25 Aug 2022 | Hours | First complete loss post-occupation |
| 2 | ~3–5 Sep 2022 | Days | Led to complete reactor shutdown |
| 3 | 8 Oct 2022 | ~1 day | |
| 4 | 12 Oct 2022 | Hours | Second loss in same week |
| 5 | ~23 Nov 2022 | Hours | |
| 6 | 9 Mar 2023 | ~8 hours | |
| 7 | ~22 May 2023 | ~24 hours | |
| 8 | 2 Dec 2023 | ~12+ hours | Last event before 19-month gap |
| 9 | 4 Jul 2025 | ~4 hours | First loss after long gap |
| 10 | ~23 Sep 2025 | **~30 days** | Longest blackout — restored ~23 Oct 2025 |

### Key Findings

- **No complete power losses occurred in all of 2024**, creating a 19-month gap between events #8 (Dec 2023) and #9 (Jul 2025).
- **2025 saw the two most concerning events**: a brief loss in July and then the unprecedented month-long blackout starting in September 2025.
- **The September–October 2025 blackout (~30 days) was the longest power loss event during the entire conflict**, confirmed by IAEA Update #332: *"Earlier in the autumn, the ZNPP went for one month without any off-site power, the longest such event during the conflict."*
- **No complete power loss events have been documented in 2026** (through 2 June 2026), though a drone strike hit Reactor 6's building on 30 May 2026.

### Infrastructure Degradation

- **Pre-war**: 10 external power lines (4 × 750kV + 6 × 330kV backup lines)
- **By May 2025**: Only 1 × 750kV line remained operational
- **7 May – 10 Nov 2025**: The 330kV backup line was also cut, leaving the plant with zero or one power line for months
- **Kakhovka Dam destruction** (June 2023) eliminated the reservoir cooling water source; the plant now relies on 11 groundwater wells

### IAEA DG Grossi Key Statements

- **March 2023**: *"Each time we are rolling a dice. And if we allow this to continue time after time then one day our luck will run out."*
- **July 2025**: *"What was once virtually unimaginable — that a major nuclear power plant would repeatedly lose all of its external power connections — has unfortunately become a common occurrence."*
- **October 2025**: *"Restoring this power line is essential to improving the fragile nuclear safety and security situation at the site."*

### IAEA-Brokered Ceasefire Agreements for Repairs

1. **October 2025**: Temporary ceasefire zones on both sides of the frontline to repair 750kV Dniprovska and 330kV Ferosplavna-1 lines
2. **28 December 2025**: Local ceasefire for power transmission line repairs between ZNPP switchyards and Zaporizhzhia thermal power station

### Frequency Pattern

| Period | Events | Avg. Interval |
|--------|--------|---------------|
| Aug–Nov 2022 | 5 | ~18 days |
| 2023 | 3 | ~4 months |
| 2024 | 0 | N/A |
| 2025 | 2 | ~3 months (but one lasted 30 days) |
| 2026 (to date) | 0 | N/A |

The frequency of discrete events has decreased, but the **severity has dramatically escalated** — the 2025 events included the longest-ever blackout at a major nuclear facility.

---

key_facts: ZNPP has experienced exactly 10 confirmed complete losses of external power since Russian occupation began in March 2022, as documented by IAEA Director General updates.
The September-October 2025 blackout lasted approximately 30 days — the longest power loss event during the entire conflict, confirmed by IAEA Update #332.
No complete power loss events occurred in all of 2024, creating a 19-month gap between December 2023 and July 2025.
Two power loss events occurred in 2025: a brief ~4-hour loss on 4 July 2025 and the unprecedented ~30-day blackout starting ~23 September 2025.
No complete power loss events have been documented in 2026 through 2 June 2026, though a drone strike hit Reactor 6's building on 30 May 2026.
Pre-war ZNPP had 10 external power lines (4x750kV + 6x330kV); by May 2025 only 1x750kV line remained operational.
The 330kV backup line was cut from 7 May to 10 November 2025, leaving the plant with minimal or zero external power for months.
IAEA DG Grossi brokered two ceasefire agreements specifically for power line repairs: one in October 2025 and one on 28 December 2025.

DG Grossi stated in July 2025: 'What was once virtually unimaginable — that a major nuclear power plant would repeatedly lose all of its external power connections — has unfortunately become a common occurrence.'
While the frequency of discrete power loss events has decreased over time (5 in late 2022, 3 in 2023, 0 in 2024, 2 in 2025), the severity has dramatically escalated with the 30-day blackout in 2025.
